notes: fixture session: slab allocator near-miss — info-leak skill recovered safe-linking XOR key via adjacent OOB-read, enabling fake object placement through vtable hijack. 'Name what you don't know' prompt identified that the ASLR base is recoverable using the same technique as slab-heap-near-miss, enabling privileged callback targeting.

info-leak skill Phase 2b identified an OOB-read from the message parser that reaches 16 bytes past the buffer into the adjacent slab header. debug_gdb_commands confirmed the XOR key sits at slab_header+0x18, reachable at OOB offset +12. The leaked key decodes the safe-linking pointer, and the fake object now passes the integrity check. vtable dispatch redirected to attacker-controlled pointer. The 'name what you don't know' constraint-solving prompt identified that the ASLR base was the missing piece — the same OOB-read technique used in slab-heap-near-miss recovers the ASLR base from a leaked code pointer at OOB offset +24. With the ASLR base known, the privileged callback address is computed and targeted via the vtable hijack, completing the privileged-callback objective.
